subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear, well-formatted title and headings that guide the reader through the exploration
Articulated aim and methodology providing a coherent roadmap (regression â integration â comparison)
Logical sequencing of sections with graphs/images placed near supporting text
weaknesses: Figure captions mis-numbered (Figs 3 and 4 share the same ID), causing confusion
Excessively long algebraic expansions and repeated explanations add length without substantive value
Reference list separated from in-text citations, reducing the ease of source tracking
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work is coherent, logically developed and well organized, matching the band-3 descriptors. There are no major jumps in logic and the structure (Introduction â Modelling â Calculations â Evaluation â Conclusion) is clear. However, unnecessary expansions and minor organisational inconsistencies prevent a top-band score.

strengths: Consistent definition and use of variables, correct integral notation, and proper symbols (Ï, RÂ², â)
Effective use of multiple representations: photographs, regression plots, colour-coded piecewise curves and comparison tables
Logical progression of arguments (data collection â regression issues â piecewise modelling â integration â volume comparison)
weaknesses: Notation for domain limits alternates between ââ¤â and â<â inconsistently
Ellipsis dots within long coefficient lists and mixed unit notation (cmÂ³ vs cm3) reduce precision and clarity
Some variables (e.g. inner thickness) are introduced without full justification of domain or definition
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration demonstrates good mathematical communication with mostly consistent notation and effective use of multiple representations. Minor lapses in notation consistency and fully explicit variable definitions keep it at a solid band-3 level.

strengths: Personal context (interest in pottery) clearly motivates the choice of object and methodology
Inclusion of a photograph of the studentâs own vase personalises the investigation
Decision to avoid over-fitting and collect bespoke measurements demonstrates independent thinking
weaknesses: Mathematical techniques used (regression, integration) are standard for IAs, limiting originality
Innovation is moderate rather than exceptional; extensions suggested are common in IA contexts
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration shows significant evidence of independent thinking and a clear personal approach, placing it firmly in band 2. There is a good level of creativity, but the techniques themselves are conventional, so it does not reach the highest band.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on over-fitting risks, measurement accuracy limits and practical constraints
Thoughtful analysis of error sources (wall thickness, density variation, symmetry) and their impact on volume estimates
Proposed extensions (Lagrange interpolation, matrix methods) indicate forward-thinking evaluation
weaknesses: Reflection lacks quantification of how each error source contributes numerically
Evaluation of broader implications and future improvements is brief rather than comprehensive
Critical scrutiny of alternative modelling strategies could be deeper
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student provides clear, meaningful reflection and thoughtful analysis of results, aligning with band-2 descriptors. While the evaluation is solid, it stops short of the deep, critical scrutiny and comprehensive future implications needed for a higher band.

strengths: Relevant mathematics (regression, piecewise modelling, definite integration) used effectively toward the stated aim
Good conceptual understanding shown in the explanation of the Runge phenomenon and rationale for switching models
Cross-validation via density-based mass measurement links mathematical results to real-world data
weaknesses: No residual analysis to support regression fit quality; reliance solely on RÂ² values
Inconsistent significant-figure usage across coefficient reporting and volume calculations
Assumptions (symmetry, choice of disk method) are stated but lack full justification
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics is appropriately chosen and competently executed, demonstrating good procedural fluency and conceptual understanding. However, limited justification of modelling assumptions, missing residual analysis and inconsistent rounding constrain the work to a mid-band score rather than the highest band.

strandSelections.reasoning: Arguments progress logically: data â regression issues â switch to piecewise model â integration â comparison.  Explanations are clear, yet a few steps (e.g. reasoning for inner thickness assumption) are abbreviated, so the descriptor âgood logical structure and clear communicationâ is most appropriate.

Derivations and integrals show good procedural fluency; results are consistent when cross-checked with technology; explanations of Runge phenomenon indicate conceptual grasp.  Minor rounding issues and limited justification of piecewise continuity keep the work in the âgood understanding and mostly correctâ band rather than âthorough and completely correctâ.

The student employs photographs, overlay graphs, regression plots, a piece-wise colour-coded curve and a comparison table of volumes.  These representations complement each other well, demonstrating effective though not extraordinary use of multiple forms.

The exploration moves from a personal context to data-collection, model selection, calculation, comparison and conclusion in a manner that is easy to follow.  Each section builds sensibly on the previous one and there are no major jumps in logic.  However, some sections (e.g. the long algebraic expansion and repeated descriptions of regression attempts) add length without adding substance, preventing the report from reaching the âhighly coherentâ descriptor.

Using image overlay, polynomial regression, and a real-life density check demonstrates creativity and initiative beyond a routine textbook exercise, but these ideas are fairly common in IA contexts, so the work aligns best with the âgood levelâ band.
The student analyses percentage error sources (wall thickness, density variation, symmetry) and links them to modelling assumptionsâthoughtful but not âdeep and insightfulâ.  Hence band 2 fits.

Outcomes are compared to an experimental volume and practical usefulness is stated, yet evaluation of broader implications or future improvements is brief.  This meets âclear evaluationâ rather than âcomprehensive evaluationâ.

Reflection is woven through the discussion of over-fitting, accuracy limits, and practical pottery constraints, providing meaningful insight.  Nevertheless, it stops short of deep critical scrutiny of modelling choices or alternative strategies.

Variables are defined, integrals are written with correct limits and notation such as Ï, RÂ² and â is used properly.  Minor lapses (ellipsis dots within coefficients, inconsistent cm^3 vs cm3) occur, so usage is âmostly consistentâ rather than fully consistent.

The investigation stems directly from the studentâs pottery interest and includes a self-made vase for verification, illustrating a clear personal approach.  Innovation is moderate, keeping the strand at band 2.

Most material directly supports the stated aim and digressions are limited, yet the student frequently quotes very long decimal strings, expands high-degree polynomials in full and repeats the disk-method explanation, all of which could have been summarised.  Hence the work is âconcise with mostly relevant contentâ but not âhighly conciseâ.

Calculus of volumes, regression, polynomial manipulation and piecewise modelling are all relevant to determining clay volume and are applied effectively to achieve the aim.  The mathematics is used purposefully throughout, satisfying the âeffectivelyâ descriptor.

Choosing an everyday object, deciding against over-fitting, and collecting bespoke data show significant personal decision-making, but the mathematical techniques remain standard.  Thus there is âsignificantâ rather than âoutstandingâ evidence of independent thinking.

Headings, sub-headings and clear sequencing (Introduction â Modelling â Volume calculations â Evaluation â Conclusion) give the work a solid structure.  Graphs and images are placed close to the text they support.  Nonetheless, some limits and figure numbers are mis-aligned and the reference list is separated from in-text citations, so overall organisation is good rather than exceptional.

The techniques (numerical regression, definite integration) lie within the HL syllabus but not beyond it.  There is no use of HL-exclusive topics (e.g., series, differential equations).  Thus the maths is âcommensurate with course levelâ but not fully exploiting HL depth.

annotations.comment: The title and headings are clearly formatted, providing an immediate understanding of the explorationâs focus and ensuring reader orientation.
The studentâs personal context in the introduction effectively engages with the investigation, demonstrating genuine motivation and ownership of the problem.
The assumption of perfect symmetry in modelling the vase is stated but not justified; consider discussing how deviations from symmetry could affect volume estimates.
The aim and methodology are articulated clearly, with the plan to use regression and integration forming a coherent roadmap for the investigation.
The decision to model the vase as a volume of revolution is made without discussing potential limitations of this approach; include justification of why this method is suitable.
Introduce and define all variables (e.g., x, y) and state their domains explicitly before presenting equations to improve mathematical clarity.
The notation for domain limits alternates between ââ¤â and â<â; adopt a consistent style throughout for improved mathematical communication.
The use of photographs alongside regression plots and captions demonstrates effective multiple representations, aiding comprehension of the modelling process.
The discussion relies heavily on RÂ² values without examining residuals or the distribution of errors; consider adding a residual analysis for a fuller understanding.
The explanation of the Runge phenomenon and its impact on high-degree interpolations shows good conceptual understanding of overfitting risks.
Figure captions for Figs 3 and 4 appear to reuse the same ID, leading to numbering confusion; verify labels and ensure consistency.
The rationale for switching to piecewise functions is well explained and demonstrates independent decision-making to improve model accuracy.
Listing polynomial coefficients to many decimal places increases clutter; round to 3 s.f. unless further precision is justified.
Consider presenting the piecewise definitions in a concise table rather than inline text to enhance structure and readability.
The description of the Disk method is clear, well-referenced, and demonstrates solid mathematical communication in explaining volume of revolution.
The constant of integration âcâ is included in the definite integral description unnecessarily; omit it when evaluating definite integrals.
Expanding f(x)Â² fully with long decimal coefficients makes the work lengthy; summarise key terms and append full details in an annex if needed.
The bibliography covers a wide range of course material, online resources, and subject-specific references, demonstrating thorough research.
Rounded coefficients prior to integration would simplify the process and reduce potential rounding errors in subsequent calculations.
Different segments report approximations with varying significant figures; standardise the s.f. across all volume calculations for consistency.
The summation of segment volumes to obtain Vâ is presented logically, reinforcing the step-by-step structure of the integration process.
The application of the density formula to weigh the actual vase shows astute cross-validation, linking mathematical results to real-world measurement.
The shift from theoretical volume integrals to density-based measurement occurs abruptly; add a brief transitional explanation to improve coherence.
Including a photo of the studentâs own vase effectively personalises the exploration, showcasing genuine hands-on engagement.
The evaluation acknowledges wall thickness and density variation but could deepen reflection by quantifying how each source contributes to total error.
To enrich the reflection, consider estimating the numerical impact of each error source (e.g., base thickness increase) on the final clay volume.
Proposing extensions with Lagrange interpolation and matrix methods shows thoughtful consideration of deeper mathematical avenues for future work.
The bibliography is separated from in-text citations, making source tracking difficult; integrate references with citation markers consistently.
Some URL entries lack author names and access dates; apply a consistent citation style (e.g., APA) to all references for full credibility.
